overview:
**High fire danger persists across the Montrose (High Elevation) area today, driven by low humidity (32%) and a high ERC of 86%.**
- Danger stays "High" through July 28, with ERC easing into the 72–82% range as the week progresses.
- As of July 21, 2026, firefighters continue battling active fires under hot, dry, and windy conditions.
- Wednesday brings a shift: showers and thunderstorms likely, with an 80% chance of precipitation, under an active Flood Watch through 3 AM Thursday.
- Watchout status: 0 of 7 local thresholds triggered today.
- Stage 2 fire restrictions remain in effect on BLM Uncompahgre, Gunnison and Tres Rios lands, the GMUG National Forest, and Black Canyon/Curecanti.
- The Gold Mountain Fire has burned 38,009 acres and remains 24% contained north of Ouray, with crews continuing structure protection.
lead: High fire danger persists across the Montrose (High Elevation) area today, driven by low humidity (32%) and a high ERC of 86%.

sources.name: Wildfire Map Spotlight: Snyder, Babylon, Ferris, and Gold Mountain Fires in Colorado and Utah | IQAir USA
